answer:
\boxed{C}

A permuterm index (or rotation index) stores all possible rotations of terms to enable efficient wildcard queries, which inherently increases the size of the dictionary due to the redundant storage of rotated versions. While options A and B may apply to some indexing structures, they are not the primary disadvantage of permuterm indexes. Option D is incorrect because permuterm indexes are specifically designed to handle misspellings or partial matches.
